questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a SKILL.md file?

options.A: To define the agent's database schema
options.B: To document reusable, self-contained agent behaviour with triggers and instructions
options.C: To store API keys and environment variables
options.D: To configure the model's temperature and token limits

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five sections of a well-structured SKILL.md?
How are skills included in an agent's system prompt at runtime?

options.A: They are uploaded to a vector database and retrieved by similarity
options.B: The content of each SKILL.md is substituted into the system prompt at placeholder positions
options.C: They are passed as separate tools in the API call
options.D: They are loaded as Python modules

questions.question: What is a state trigger in the context of agent skills?

options.A: A trigger based on the agent's model weights
options.B: A trigger that activates based on conversation state, such as the user finishing a chapter
options.C: A trigger based on database state
options.D: A trigger that fires every time the agent is initialised

questions.question: What is the main advantage of decomposing agent behaviour into skills vs a monolithic system prompt?

options.A: Skills reduce the model's context window usage to zero
options.B: Skills eliminate the need for a system prompt entirely
options.C: Skills are modular, independently testable, and reusable across agents

questions.question: A 'keyword trigger' in a SKILL.md activates the skill when:

options.A: The conversation has lasted more than 10 turns
options.B: The user message contains specific words or phrases like 'quiz me' or 'test me'
options.C: The model confidence drops below a threshold
options.D: The user's account tier is Pro

questions.question: In the Concept Explainer skill, how should the explanation depth be chosen?

options.A: Always use the most technical explanation possible
options.B: Based on the user's vocabulary and prior messages to assess their level
options.C: Always use an analogy first regardless of user level

questions.question: Which of the following represents a multi-turn skill workflow?

options.A: A single response that answers a question
options.B: A sequence of agent actions spanning multiple messages, like a 5-question quiz session
options.C: A batch of API calls made in parallel
options.D: A cron job that runs daily

questions.question: What constraint does the Concept Explainer skill impose on explanation scope?

options.A: Always explain at least 3 related concepts
options.B: Do not explain more than one concept per response

questions.question: In a skill composition system prompt, how are multiple skills combined?

options.A: Each skill gets its own separate API call
options.B: Skills are listed in a config file and loaded at startup
options.C: Skill content is substituted at placeholder positions in a master system prompt
options.D: Skills are merged into a single tool definition

questions.question: What should the agent do when no skill matches the user's message?

options.B: Respond helpfully and guide the user back to the course content
options.C: Refuse to answer and ask the user to rephrase

questions.question: The Quiz Master skill's workflow ends by:

options.A: Redirecting the user to a different website
options.B: Giving a final score, highlighting weak areas, and suggesting re-reading if score < 60%
options.D: Sending a score report via email

questions.question: Which file structure is recommended for organising agent skills?

options.A: All skills in a single skills.py file
options.B: Each skill in its own subdirectory under skills/ with a SKILL.md file
options.C: Skills stored in a relational database
options.D: Skills embedded directly in the agent's main script

questions.question: An 'intent trigger' differs from a 'keyword trigger' because it:

options.A: Only works with paid API tiers
options.B: Requires the user to use exact trigger phrases
options.C: Activates based on inferred user intent, even without specific trigger words
options.D: Fires based on the time of day

questions.question: What is the key benefit of the Output Format section in a SKILL.md?

options.A: It sets the JSON schema for tool calls
options.B: It ensures consistent, structured responses that meet user expectations
options.C: It controls the model's sampling temperature
options.D: It limits the number of tokens in each response
